Verification log 2026-05-02 DESKTOP_SEARCH_VISUAL_PARITY_REAL_ENTITY_IMAGES_AND_FULL_VOICE_SMOKE_BUILD_V3: AGENTS.md was updated with the narrow Visual Search Presentation and Live Voice Proof law. Kernel `SearchImagePacket` validation now supports approved remote display only with explicit remote-load approval, safe public image/thumbnail URL, safe source-page URL, display-policy approval, and no raw-image source-page click target. PH1.E now strips generic trailing location qualifiers from role entity queries, builds generic role-aware provider queries, blocks weak/thin directory evidence generically, preserves wrong-entity rejection, extracts person names around role claims without real searched-name hardcoding, and can augment accepted-source answers with source-bound Brave image metadata only when remote image display and image search gates are explicitly enabled. Adapter transport preserves `image_cards` separately from answer/TTS text and admits remote cards only under the same policy gates. Desktop decodes bounded approved image cards, renders actual approved image/thumbnail URLs through the Swift view with placeholder only during loading/failure, removes the old raw URL source dump rows from normal provenance, and styles accepted source chips as compact rounded gray/shadow pills. Controlled live Brave typed probes used the configured key without printing it and stayed below the JD cap, but real public role/entity prompts safe-degraded instead of returning accepted source chips/images; this is recorded as a live search-quality blocker, not hidden. Post-live provider-off proof passed with all provider flags OFF, `/healthz` healthy, startup provider probe blocked before provider use, process-scoped `lsof` showing only `127.0.0.1:18080 (LISTEN)`, and a provider-disabled search answer. Desktop UI/voice proof was attempted after build: intended spoken prompt `Explain what Selene is.` was captured as `Explain what Celine is.` and answered as a fashion-brand query, so English voice proof is invalid and Chinese voice proof was not run in this build; typed proof is not counted as voice proof.

Summary update: H363 is now implemented as the post-H362 PH1.E deterministic time clarification-continuity build through [ph1e.rs#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_engines/src/ph1e.rs#L1), [ph1x.rs#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/ph1x.rs#L1), [lib.rs#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_adapter/src/lib.rs#L1), and [H363_PH1E_DETERMINISTIC_TIME_CLARIFICATION_CONTINUITY_AND_SAME_THREAD_FOLLOWUP_COMPLETION_BUILD_PLAN.md#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/docs/PHASE_PLANS/PHASE_H_IMPLEMENTATION/H363_PH1E_DETERMINISTIC_TIME_CLARIFICATION_CONTINUITY_AND_SAME_THREAD_FOLLOWUP_COMPLETION_BUILD_PLAN.md#L1). H363 turns incomplete, ambiguous, and multi-timezone deterministic time prompts into clean clarifications; keeps the pending deterministic time request bound to the same thread; and completes follow-up place replies globally without governance/runtime out-of-sync. Spain and Portugal are examples of the generic clarification-continuity rule, not the full scope. No shell/sidebar/composer redesign, PH1.D redesign, provider swap, backend route expansion, protected-action law widening, fake local answer lane, or web-search fallback for deterministic time is introduced. Proof: `cargo check -p selene_engines -p selene_os -p selene_adapter`; targeted H363 Rust tests; H362 and adapter deterministic regressions; protected-action identity-gate regression; `git diff --check`; and live `selene_adapter_http` `/v1/voice/turn` proof returned clean clarifications and same-thread follow-up completions for Spain/Madrid, Portugal/Lisbon, United States/Los Angeles, Australia/Perth, Springfield/Springfield Illinois, and America/New_York with no raw ISO, web-search dump, raw provider payload, or governance out-of-sync leakage.

The new Desktop input path is feature-flagged off by default, mints short-lived OpenAI Realtime transcription client secrets through the adapter, streams bounded foreground microphone audio as PCM16 chunks, observes OpenAI transcript deltas during speech, commits one final transcript, and hands that final transcript to existing `/v1/voice/turn` authority. The `SELENE_DESKTOP_OPENAI_TTS_ENABLED` feature flag defaults off; when enabled for explicit Desktop voice replies, Selene renders the final runtime answer text first, then the backend calls OpenAI `/v1/audio/speech` with `gpt-4o-mini-tts`, voice `marin`, and MP3 output using the existing server-side `openai_api_key` without exposing the permanent key to Swift. The backend returns answer and audio digests, byte length, model, voice, AI-voice disclosure, bounded request/cost failure behavior, and redacted safe failures. Desktop plays returned audio in memory through `AVAudioPlayer`, rejects stale audio by generation/turn/hash, cancels playback or pending TTS on newer turns or mic press, keeps transcript text independent of TTS success, and does not retain local playback fallback; explicit flag-off or failure paths fail closed and are not counted as Build 1B success. Proof: managed Desktop adapter direct `/v1/voice/turn` returned clean joke and Tokyo time answers; `/v1/desktop/openai-tts/speech` returned `OPENAI_TTS_SPEECH_CREATED` with matching `answer_text_sha256`, matching `audio_sha256`, nonzero `audio_byte_len`, no key exposure, and `synthetic_ai_voice` disclosure; JD-assisted live Desktop proof for `Tell me a joke`, `What time is it in Tokyo`, and `Tell me about space` showed realtime transcription session creation, audio commit, first delta, `/v1/voice/turn` handoff, `TTS_REQUESTING`, `TTS_READY`, and `PLAYING_OPENAI fallback_used=false`; JD reported the run worked well with only expected non-streaming synthesis delay after text render. No image/photo card implementation, GDELT live implementation, DOCX/PDF export, company/internal knowledge search, Build 3, protected execution, simulation bypass, authority bypass, fake provider fanout, fake multihop, fake citations, fake reports, or raw full-page archive is introduced.

Summary update: H390 is now implemented as the Brave image display-safety/license/source-page verification slice. H390 preserves H389 `Outcome B -- APPROVED_BRAVE_IMAGE_METADATA_ONLY_PATH` and adds display eligibility metadata rather than UI display. PH1.E emits an `ImageDisplayEligibilityPacket`-equivalent field with source-page verification status, minimal HTML metadata signals, explicit license/usage signal, robots/noimageindex policy, display eligibility, display-deferred or blocked reason, no-image-bytes-downloaded proof, no-raw-image-cache proof, no-source-page-scrape proof, query-hash-only proof, and text-citation-required proof; the adapter exposes this as structured `deep_research.image_display_eligibility` metadata. The default live Brave path remains `Outcome B -- DISPLAY_DEFERRED_LICENSE_OR_SAFETY_INCOMPLETE` because explicit license/usage and display-safety proof remain incomplete. H390 does not emit `WEB_IMAGE_SOURCE_CARD_PASS`, does not display image cards, does not modify Desktop Swift/UI/chrome, does not download image bytes, does not cache raw image bytes, does not create a hidden image archive, does not add a provider/dependency, and does not replace text citations with images.

Summary update: H391 is now implemented as the Brave image provider-policy and display-rights decision slice. H391 preserves H390 `Outcome B -- DISPLAY_DEFERRED_LICENSE_OR_SAFETY_INCOMPLETE` and adds a provider-agnostic `ImageProviderDisplayPolicy`-equivalent metadata surface exposed as `deep_research.image_provider_display_policy`. H391 selects `Outcome B -- BRAVE_IMAGE_DISPLAY_POLICY_METADATA_AND_SOURCE_LINK_ONLY`: bounded metadata and source-page links may remain available when source URL/domain proof exists, but thumbnail display, full image display, sourced image/photo card display, and `WEB_IMAGE_SOURCE_CARD_PASS` remain blocked/deferred because official Brave docs do not explicitly prove publisher rights, thumbnail display rights, or full image display rights. H391 records official Brave docs reviewed, attribution requirement, storage/cache limits, publisher-rights requirement, unknown-license behavior, H392 handoff, no UI display, no Desktop Swift/UI/chrome modification, no image-byte download, no raw image cache, no new provider/dependency, and no replacement of text citations. Brave-specific mapping is isolated to the Brave provider policy logic and H391 proof classes; generic packets and adapter response fields remain provider-neutral for future providers.

Remaining gap: if the provider commits only the first clause and no partial-transcript evidence contains the missing continuation, runtime cannot infer the uncommitted words and must treat the committed transcript as the available truth.

  Stage 2 provider-control implementation update: Stage 2 is implemented as the global search-provider kill switch, budget/counter, and billing-grade usage/cost metadata foundation after H415B. [AGENTS.md#L44](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/AGENTS.md#L44) now requires the provider kill switch, budget/counter before calls, disabled providers as zero-attempt/zero-network/non-billable, and usage/cost events with account layer, tenant/company/private-user ownership, actor, service/module, provider, route, operation type, cost owner, billing scope, and billable class where runtime context exists.

  Stage 3 implementation update: Stage 3 is implemented as the safe page fetch/read and evidence extraction foundation after H415B and Stage 2, with prerequisite commits `9561861fa421c09bacac48219c5b63bba0d1f5ba` and `ed414875d65fd6051bdb841025e6a870732ca97a` proven as current-HEAD ancestors before edits. Safe URL admission now blocks unsafe schemes, credentials, localhost, private/internal/link-local/reserved/metadata hosts, IPv4-mapped private IPv6, and unsafe redirects at [admission.rs#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/url/admission.rs#L1), [canonical.rs#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/url/canonical.rs#L1), and [fetch.rs#L55](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/url/fetch.rs#L55). URL fetch remains globally gated by Stage 2 provider/url-fetch policy; live external fetch fails closed with zero attempts and zero network dispatches when DNS/private-address validation cannot be proven, while tests use in-memory fixture transport instead of localhost HTTP at [mod.rs#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/url/mod.rs#L1) and [fetch.rs#L174](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/url/fetch.rs#L174). Evidence extraction strips script/style/noscript/nav/footer/aside noise and emits bounded source-tied evidence chunks with hard limits of 500 excerpt characters, 5 chunks per source, and 2,000 trace-preview characters at [extract.rs#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/url/extract.rs#L1), [mod.rs#L16](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/url/mod.rs#L16), [fetch.rs#L323](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/url/fetch.rs#L323), and [planning.rs#L1](/Users/selene/Documents/Selene-OS/crates/selene_os/src/web_search_plan/planning/mod.rs#L1). Extracted evidence can be preferred over provider snippets, but it does not bypass H415B accepted/rejected source rules; raw full page text is not stored, response text and TTS remain clean, and durable evidence ledger persistence remains deferred unless a later build adds recoverable storage.

  Append-only best available answer policy log (2026-05-02): `SEARCH_BEST_AVAILABLE_ANSWER_POLICY_AND_CONFLICT_RESOLUTION_REPAIR` implemented the public websearch best-available answer policy after Stage 9. PH1.E now keeps internal verification/confidence/contradiction state for trace, but user-facing public answers select the clearest accepted source-backed result instead of stopping at a generic conflict dead end. Equal accepted-source conflict returns a natural mixed-evidence answer with accepted source chips; exact requested role absence returns the closest source-backed role/title instead of an empty response; stale accepted evidence returns a useful non-current caveat; weak/wrong/rejected sources still cannot support final facts. PH1.X fallback without verification metadata now reports the closest search result title without snippets, URLs, confidence labels, raw classes, or source dumps. Desktop renders accepted source chips as compact clickable pills directly under the authoritative reply text and in the provenance panel, matching the required small source-link target while remaining non-authoritative and provider-secret-free. Protected execution remains simulation/authority gated and cannot use best-available guessing.

  Append-only canonical Stage 4A implementation log (2026-05-03): `STAGE_4A_ACTIVATION_SESSION_TURN_AND_PACKET_FOUNDATION_RECONCILIATION` completed the narrowed packet foundation after Stage 3A. Existing runtime ingress/session carriers were preserved and not rebuilt. `Stage4ActivationPacket`, `Stage4ActivationSource`, `Stage4TurnBoundaryPacket`, `Stage4TurnBoundaryKind`, `Stage4RecordBoundary`, `Stage4RecordingState`, and `Stage4PacketRouteAuthority` now provide the minimal packet-boundary carrier in `runtime_ingress_turn_foundation.rs`. Packet creation carries activation source, platform posture, session hint, consent/device/provider-budget/audit references, candidate/committed/record boundaries, and record artifact fields while proving zero route authority for tools, search, providers, TTS, or protected execution. Record-button activation is artifact-only, cannot create a committed live turn, and cannot carry route, modality, turn ID, or device turn sequence state. Canonical roadmap packet names were crosswalked to repo-truth carriers instead of duplicated: activation/turn/record packet names map to the Stage 4A carrier plus runtime ingress/session/PH1.F/PH1.J truth; `SessionPacket` maps to PH1.L/session projections and permits; `ConsentStatePacket`, `DeviceTrustPacket`, and `ProviderBudgetPacket` map to Stage 3A consent, `PlatformRuntimeContext.device_trust_class`, and PH1.COST/PH1.QUOTA/provider-control carriers. Wake detection, live voice, STT/TTS, Voice ID matching, native UI, and the Stage 27 record product remain deferred. Stage 5A is ready to start as session open/resume/close, runtime turn spine, and stale-turn quarantine reconciliation.

  Append-only canonical Stage 5A implementation log (2026-05-03): `STAGE_5A_SESSION_OPEN_RESUME_CLOSE_RUNTIME_TURN_SPINE_AND_STALE_TURN_QUARANTINE_RECONCILIATION` completed the narrowed session and runtime-turn authority layer after Stage 4A. Existing PH1.L and `RuntimeSessionFoundation` session lifecycle, permit, commit, retry, single-writer, deferral, closed-session, and device-timeline carriers were preserved as repo truth and not rebuilt. `Stage5TurnAuthorityPacket`, `Stage5TurnAuthorityDisposition`, and `Stage5TurnWorkAuthority` now prove current committed turn authority and quarantine state in `runtime_session_foundation.rs`. `authorize_stage5_current_committed_turn` rejects mismatched permits, closed sessions, newer deferred turns, and non-current device timelines before later understanding can consume the turn. Quarantined stale, superseded, cancelled, abandoned, closed-session, and record-artifact-only dispositions cannot enter understanding, render as current, or route tools, search, providers, TTS, connector work, or protected execution. Stage 5A targeted tests prove current-turn admission, newer deferred-turn quarantine, older completed-turn quarantine after a newer commit, and no-route authority for quarantine dispositions; broad conversation-control, clarification, correction, recovery, same-page state, and natural turn-taking/backchannel work remain deferred to Stage 5B. Stage 5B is the next exact build.
